default_description: Approved SQLite database available for read-only questions. Detailed tables, columns, and remembered database context are available separately when this database is selected.
default_description_short: Approved SQLite database available for read-only local-data questions.

user_actions.manage_needed: No approved databases are available. Invite the user to manage databases using manage_href.

recovery_guidance.database_query_unavailable_database_id: Use database_list_info to choose an available database_id before querying the database.
recovery_guidance.database_query_missing_database_id: Use database_list_info before querying the database.
recovery_guidance.database_context_read_unavailable_databa: Use database_list_info to choose an available database_id before reading database context.
recovery_guidance.database_context_read_missing_database_i: Use database_list_info before reading database context.
recovery_guidance.helper_database_info_remember_unavailabl: Use database_list_info to choose an available database_id before remembering database info.
recovery_guidance.helper_database_info_remember_missing_da: Use database_list_info before remembering database info.

database_descriptions.description_short: Apple Messages database for SMS/iMessage conversations, participants, message text, timestamps, and attachments.
database_descriptions.description: Apple Messages SMS/iMessage database for conversations, participants, message text, delivery/read timestamps, chat membership, and attachment metadata. Use this database for message and conversation questions. Message identity is in handle.id and chat_handle_join.handle_id; there is usually no direct foreign key to Contacts. When the user asks who a phone number or email belongs to, also use AddressBook.sqlitedb and match normalized handle.id values against contact phone/email values.

database_descriptions.description_short: Apple Contacts address book for names, organizations, phone numbers, emails, addresses, birthdays, groups, and notes.
database_descriptions.description: Apple Contacts address book database for people, organizations, names, phone numbers, email addresses, postal addresses, URLs, birthdays, notes, groups, accounts, and contact search keys. Use this database for contact identity and contact-detail questions and as the identity-resolution companion for Messages, Mail, Calendar, and other databases that only store phone numbers, email addresses, or display names. Contact details join from ABPerson.ROWID to ABMultiValue.record_id; in typical iOS AddressBook data ABMultiValue.property 3 is phone, 4 is email, and 5 is postal address, but sample values if uncertain.

database_descriptions.description_short: Apple Contacts image database for contact thumbnails and full-size contact photos.
database_descriptions.description: Apple Contacts image database for contact thumbnails and full-size contact photos stored as blobs. Use this database only when a contact photo is relevant; pair it with AddressBook.sqlitedb to resolve record ids to names and contact details.

database_descriptions.description_short: Apple Calendar database for calendars, events, recurrences, attendees, organizers, alarms, notes, and locations.
database_descriptions.description: Apple Calendar database for calendars, stores/accounts, events, all-day flags, start and end dates, locations, notes/descriptions, recurrences, exception dates, alarms, attendees, organizers, invitations, and occurrence caches. Use this database for schedule and event questions. For attendee or organizer identity, also use AddressBook.sqlitedb: Calendar participants and identities store email addresses and names, while Contacts maps those emails to people and richer contact details. Contact-backed birthday/location fields may also need Contacts context.

database_descriptions.description_short: Apple Calendar supplemental database for reminder settings, notification settings, and acknowledged alarm metadata.
database_descriptions.description: Apple Calendar supplemental database for reminder/notification settings and acknowledged alarm metadata. Use this as supporting context for Calendar.sqlitedb, not as the primary event source.

database_descriptions.description_short: Apple Mail metadata index for mailboxes, messages, dates, flags, folder placement, threads, and cached message metadata.
database_descriptions.description: Apple Mail metadata index for mailboxes, messages, dates, flags, message-to-mailbox placement, threads, and cached message data. Use this database for folder and message metadata questions. Pair it with Protected Index for sender, recipient, subject, and protected content fields. When the user asks who an email address belongs to, also use AddressBook.sqlitedb and match sender/recipient email strings to contact email values.

database_descriptions.description_short: Apple Mail protected content index for senders, recipients, subjects, body fragments, and protected message data.
database_descriptions.description: Apple Mail protected content index for sender, recipient, subject, body fragments, and protected message data. Use this database for bounded body-text snippets or content lookups. Pair it with Envelope Index for dates, mailbox placement, flags, and thread context. When sender, _to, cc, or bcc need human contact names, also use AddressBook.sqlitedb and match email strings to contact email values.

database_descriptions.description_short: Apple Mail recent-address database for recently used email addresses, display names, sending addresses, and last-used dates.
database_descriptions.description: Apple Mail recent-address database for recently used email addresses, display names, sending addresses, bundle identifiers, and last-used dates. Use this for autocomplete/recent-recipient questions, not for message contents. Recent addresses are email strings, so use AddressBook.sqlitedb when the question needs full contact identity or contact details.

database_descriptions.description_short: Apple Photos metadata database for photo/video assets, albums, filenames, dimensions, dates, identifiers, comments, and keywords.
database_descriptions.description: Apple Photos library metadata database for photo and video assets, albums, album membership, filenames, dimensions, media type, creation/modification dates, local/cloud identifiers, shared-album comments, keywords, sidecars, and adjustment metadata. Use this database for photo-library metadata questions; it does not contain the original image or video bytes.

database_descriptions.description_short: Apple Photos supplemental database for model-interest counters and internal asset/album activity tracking.
database_descriptions.description: Apple Photos supplemental activity/change-counter database for model-interest and asset/album activity tracking. Use this only as supporting context for Photos.sqlite when activity counters or internal change tracking are relevant.

database_descriptions.description_short: Apple Photos change-tracking database that is usually not useful for photo content questions.
database_descriptions.description: Apple Photos change-tracking database. In the inspected sample it contains no user tables, so it is usually not useful for answering photo-library content questions. Prefer Photos.sqlite for asset and album metadata.

database_descriptions.description_short: ENIL local messaging database for account data, contacts, chats, message text, read receipts, media identifiers, and event logs.
database_descriptions.description: ENIL local messaging database for account/profile data, contacts, chats, message boxes, message text and metadata, read receipts, stickers, sticons, media identifiers, and event logs. Use this database for ENIL chat, contact, and message questions; join messages with chats and contacts for human-readable conversation context.

database_descriptions.description_short: ENIL URL cache database for cached network requests and responses, usually not the primary chat data source.
database_descriptions.description: ENIL URL cache database for cached network responses. In the inspected sample it has no user tables, so it is normally not useful for chat questions. Prefer enil.sqlite for messages, contacts, chats, and stickers.

database_descriptions.description_short: Reeder metadata database for accounts, services, feeds, folders, articles, unread/starred state, and stream membership.
database_descriptions.description: Reeder RSS/reading app metadata database for accounts, services, feeds, folders, articles/items, unread/starred state, cached-item flags, publish dates, and stream membership. Use this database for feed lists, article metadata, read/unread state, and starred-item questions. Pair it with rkit-data.db for article body text.

database_descriptions.description_short: Reeder article-content database for item titles, authors, links, extracted content, and readability content.
database_descriptions.description: Reeder RSS/reading app article-content database for item titles, authors, links, extracted content, and readability content. Use this database when article body text or extracted article content is needed; pair it with rkit.db for feed, folder, item status, and date context.

database_descriptions.description_short: Reeder edit/sync-queue database for pending local changes stored as JSON.
database_descriptions.description: Reeder RSS/reading app edit/sync-queue database for pending local changes stored as JSON. In the inspected sample it is empty, so it is usually only useful when diagnosing unsynced edits or local action queues.

database_descriptions.description_short: Reeder backup database for backed-up users, accounts, streams, feeds, folders, and starred article records.
database_descriptions.description: Reeder RSS/reading app backup database for backed-up users/accounts, streams/feeds, folders, and starred article records. Use this as recovery or historical context, not as the primary live article store.

database_descriptions.description_short: Reeder URL cache database for cached HTTP requests, responses, receiver data, and blobs.
database_descriptions.description: Reeder URL cache database for cached HTTP requests, responses, receiver data, and blobs. Use this only for cache/debug questions or when the primary Reeder databases do not contain needed cached response data. Prefer rkit.db and rkit-data.db for feed and article questions.
